author: @ClownWhisper
text: you know i often wonder if there's such a thing as genetic memory. Every time I see different areas in England they seem so familiar to me even when I was there I seemed to know which way to go to find places. I mean it was really bizarre the group I was with a bunch of coworkers even commented on it asking me how many times I have been there and it was my first time. I mean my father talked about London proper a lot of the time you missed England you went to school in London and I could tell he missed it but as far as pictures and things like that just the random picture would come up I didn't have a large catalog of imagery to go by anyway thanks for the video Robert very interesting

author: @TimHollingworth
text: Glad you have an open mind to ghosts. I find the subject fascinating after seeing my first ghost in a Middelsborough hotel.

replies.author: @TimHollingworth
replies.text:
@Charle De gaulle I was working for an oil company as a freelance cameraman, and stayed in a hotel in Middlesborogh. In the middle of the night I heard the door open and I sat bolt upright in bed. I saw an old woman come through the bedroom door which was in a different place in the wall.

She walked up to and around my bed and then bent over by the wall. She seemed to be getting something out of an invisible bedside cabinet that was further away from my bed. 

She looked kind of translucent with a soft glow, exactly like the watermark face on the old £1 note. 

I remember my back was tingling up and down with waves of shivers and I dove under the duvet  and couldn't move for fear for a long time. 

That was back in the late 80's and I still get a shiver in my spine just thinking about it.
